content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Role Reversal Prompting to Attenuate Social Bias in Language Models",
  "problem": "Current prompting techniques often attempt to reduce social biases in LLMs by direct instruction or filtering, but these approaches struggle to generalize and can miss subtle stereotypes. There is a need for prompting methods that more robustly disrupt stereotypical associations without explicit supervision.",
  "motivation": "Language models can perpetuate and amplify social biases present in training data, leading to harmful outputs in real-world applications. Existing mitigation strategies often fail to address implicit bias or adapt to novel forms of stereotyping. A prompting method that actively disrupts learned associations could provide a more generalizable and scalable solution.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual Role Reversal Prompting (CRRP), a technique that explicitly inverts the typical social roles or attributes associated with entities in prompts (e.g., changing gender, ethnicity, or profession) and aggregates model outputs across these counterfactuals. The prompt pool for each input is automatically generated using a controlled perturbation of named entities and descriptors, guided by a bias lexicon and co-reference resolution. Final outputs are derived by ensembling (e.g., majority vote or embedding averaging) over the set of counterfactual completions, diluting stereotypical associations. This method can be integrated as a wrapper around any generation or classification task.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CRRP on bias-sensitive datasets such as StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, and WinoBias, comparing against standard prompting and recent bias mitigation approaches (e.g., instruction tuning, self-debiasing prompts). Metrics include bias scores, accuracy, and stereotyping rate. Statistical significance will be assessed via permutation tests."
}
